100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 4, 1926, a general strike in Britain escalated, with workers across various industries walking off the job to protest wage cuts and poor working conditions. The situation prompted government discussions on potential intervention, as the strike threatened to paralyze the economy. Meanwhile, in the United States, Congress continued to debate immigration restrictions amidst rising tensions surrounding the influx of foreign workers. Internationally, tensions remained high in the Middle East as nationalist movements gained momentum against colonial rule.
Updated: Monday, May 4, 2026 at 08:14:28 AM CDT
